item.title: backlog history command for querying completed sprint items
item.intent: Users must be able to query what was completed and when without parsing JSONL by hand — sprint history should be as discoverable as the backlog itself.
item.description: The archive/sprints.jsonl file records every completed sprint (itemId, date, status, duration, filesChanged, etc.) but there is no CLI command to query it. Users who want to know 'what did I complete this week?' or 'how many items passed vs failed?' must read raw JSONL. A 'backlog history' subcommand (or 'aishore history') should let users list recent completions, filter by date range or status, and surface per-item summaries. The metrics command gives aggregates; history would give the item-level view.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find the archive path (SPRINT_ARCHIVE or sprints.jsonl) to understand the JSONL schema
Add a 'history' subcommand in the backlog command dispatcher (cmd_backlog or main case statement)

Implement cmd_backlog_history(): read sprints.jsonl, parse each line with jq, support --since YYYY-MM-DD filter (skip entries with date < arg), support --failed filter (skip entries with status == complete)
Format output as a table: ID, date, status, duration columns; align columns for readability
Add --failed flag handling: show only rows where status \!= complete
Add 'backlog history' to the help text and usage message
acceptanceCriteria: backlog history (or aishore history) lists completed sprint items with ID, date, and status
backlog history --since YYYY-MM-DD filters to items completed on or after that date
backlog history --failed shows only items where status \!= complete
